nodes.label: Evidence Gaps Summary
observations: TOTAL: 382 investigation gaps identified across the 909 active source documents.
PORTFOLIO SNAPSHOTS (317 gaps, 83%): These represent Israel Bonds positions appearing in quarterly inventory reports and custodial statements without accompanying decision-making context.
EMPTY DOCUMENTS (38 gaps, 10%): Documents that failed text extraction — scanned images, protected PDFs, or corrupted files.
UNMATCHED FIGURES (20 gaps, 5%): Dollar amounts appearing in documents that could not be matched to specific transactions or authorizations.
TIMELINE GAPS (7 gaps, 2%): All 7 gaps reference pre-2008 dates (1935-2008) and appear to derive from Israel Bonds historical marketing materials rather than Arkansas-specific operational records.

AGGREGATE PICTURE: The dominant gap type (portfolio_snapshot at 83%) tells a consistent story: the investigation has substantial evidence of WHAT was purchased and WHEN, but almost no evidence of W...
WHAT'S STILL MISSING AFTER 909 DOCS: (1) Board of Finance deliberation records for Treasury purchases.
